name: Chocolate-Orange Fruitcake with Pecans
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Chopped dried black mission figs (packed)
1/2 cup Frozen orange juice concentrate; thawed
1/4 cup Grand Marnier or other orange liqueur
6 ounces Bittersweet or semisweet chocolate; coarsely chop
1 pound Bittersweet (not sweetened) or semisweet chocolate
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 325 degrees F. Position the rack in the bottom third of the oven.
Generously butter and flour a 12-cup angel food cake pan.
Combine toasted pecans, chopped dried figs, prunes, dates, orange juice concentrate, Grand Marnier, and grated orange peel in a large bowl. Let stand for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a medium bowl, sift together the flour, cocoa, cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
In a processor, combine brown sugar and chocolate; chop into small pieces. Using an electric mixer, beat butter and cream cheese in a large bowl until blended. Add the chocolate mixture and beat until fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time. Beat in prune butter. Stir in 1/4 of the dry ingredients. Mix in the fruit mixture and remaining dry ingredients in three additions each.
Transfer the batter to the prepared pan. Bake until a tester inserted near the center with a few moist crumbs attached, about 1 hour and 55 minutes. Cool for 5 minutes. Turn the pan over onto a rack; let stand for 5 minutes. Lift off the pan; cool the cake completely. Wrap the cake in plastic and store at room temperature for up to 2 days.
For Glaze: Melt butter in a heavy medium saucepan over low heat. Add chocolate; stir until melted and smooth. Whisk in orange juice concentrate.
Place the cake on a rack. Spread some of the chocolate glaze thickly over the top and sides of the cake. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Spread the remaining chocolate glaze over the cake, covering completely. Sprinkle with chopped candied fruit peel, if desired. Refrigerate for an additional 30 minutes to set the glaze.
Fruitcake can be prepared up to 3 weeks ahead. Wrap the cake in plastic and refrigerate.
